title: Carlos Alcaraz Will Miss the French Open Due to Wrist Injury
summary: Bad news for tennis fans! Carlos Alcaraz, the 22-year-old superstar who won the French Open the last two years, won't be playing next month. Why? He hurt his right wrist during a match in Barcelona last week. After tests, doctors said he needs to rest. So Alcaraz is pulling out of both the Italian Open and the French Open (also called Roland Garros). "This is a difficult time for me, but I am sure we will come out of this stronger," he wrote on Instagram. Alcaraz has won seven Grand Slam titles (the biggest tournaments in tennis). He even won the Australian Open earlier this year! That made him the youngest man ever to win all four Grand Slams at least once. His big rival, Jannik Sinner, is now the world number one. Sinner said he was sad to hear the news and hopes Alcaraz can be back for Wimbledon. "We need to look at our bodies first," Sinner said. Alcaraz has only missed one Grand Slam before — the 2023 Australian Open because of a hamstring injury. Let's hope he heals fast!
why_it_matters: This reminds us that even top athletes get injured and need to rest—just like we have to take breaks when we're hurt, so our bodies can heal.

keywords.explanation: A very famous and successful player.

Deciding not to take part in an event after planning to.

What injury caused Alcaraz to miss the 2023 Australian Open?

background_read: Carlos Alcaraz (a 22-year-old tennis star from Spain) became the youngest man ever to win all four Grand Slam tournaments at least once earlier this year.
The French Open (also called Roland Garros) is a famous clay-court tournament in Paris that Alcaraz won the last two years.
Jannik Sinner (Alcaraz's rival from Italy) is now ranked number one in the world and said he feels sorry for his competitor.
Article_Structure: WHO: Carlos Alcaraz, a 22-year-old tennis superstar who has won seven Grand Slams.
WHAT: He is pulling out of the French Open and Italian Open because of a right wrist injury.
WHERE: The injury happened during a match in Barcelona, and the tournaments he'll miss are in Italy and France.
WHY: Doctors told him he needs to rest so he can heal properly and come back stronger.

perspectives.description: He is disappointed but believes he will come back stronger, and he is following doctors' advice to rest.
He feels sad for Alcaraz and hopes his rival recovers soon, because competition is better when both are healthy.
Fans are upset because they were excited to see Alcaraz defend his title, but they understand he needs to take care of his body.
